titleStmt.title: SII 9.193: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 193. (A.R. No. 700 of 1920.) ON PILLAR OF THE TEMPLE OF ANANTABHOGESVARA AT KOLLUR, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: The stone is mutilated and the beginning and the end of the record are lost. It is dated the cyclic year Manmatha, the 40th year of the reign of the Chālukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva, probably Vikramāditya VI (= A.D. 1115-16). The record refers to the brave Brāhman general Anantapālayya and mentions officers such as Mantri, Purōhita, Sēnāpati, Dauvārika, Yuvarāja, Rāshṭrakūṭa and Kuṭumbin in whose presence a gift was made by the king. The language of the record is partly Sanskrit.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
prasannaṁ nuḍidumattennaṁ gōtrapavitraṁ pa
nāputraṁ turagarēvantaṁ vairikritāntaṁ
stapraśastisahita śrīmadanaṁtapāladē
va tribhuvanamalladēvaścatvāriṁśa
rājyasaṁvatsarē manmathasamākhyē U
nayananimitta pauṣyaśuddaikādaśī śa
rē tribhōgābhyantaraṁ sarvvakārapari
maṁtripurōhitasēnāpa
dauvārikayuvarājyarāṣṭrakūṭakuṭuṁbi
samakṣaṁ tribhuvanamalladēvēna dattamiti vidita
mastu catuścatvāriṁśatpramāṇa māna The inscription is broken after this.
Digital edition of SII 9.193 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
